name: 植物的主要类群
subject: biology
curriculum_points: 根据生物的形态结构、生理功能以及繁殖方式等，可以将生物分为不同的类群。
藻类是能够进行光合作用的结构简单的生物。苔藓、蕨类、裸子植物和被子植物都具有根、茎、叶等器官的分化，植物繁殖过程逐渐摆脱了对水环境的依赖。
通过列表等多种方式，对不同生物类群的形态结构、生活环境、繁殖方式等进行比较，帮助学生逐步形成生物具有多样性和统一性的认识。

excerpts.text: 根据生物的形态结构、生理功能以及繁殖方式等，可以将生物分为不同的类群。
藻类是能够进行光合作用的结构简单的生物。苔藓、蕨类、裸子植物和被子植物都具有根、茎、叶等器官的分化，植物繁殖过程逐渐摆脱了对水环境的依赖。
通过列表等多种方式，对不同生物类群的形态结构、生活环境、繁殖方式等进行比较，帮助学生逐步形成生物具有多样性和统一性的认识。

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 植物的分类（藻→被子）

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物
- **学段**: 初中
- **年级**: 八年级
- **章节**: 生物多样性与进化
- **知识点**: 植物的分类（藻→被子）
- **课标版本**: 义务教育课程标准（2022年版）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 根据生物的形态结构、生理功能以及繁殖方式等，可以将生物分为不同的类群. 从苔藓植物、蕨类植物，到种子植物，逐渐出现根、茎、叶等器官的分化，植物繁殖过程逐渐摆脱了对水环境的依赖.

### 1.2 学业要求
> 对于给定的一组生物，尝试根据一定的特征对其进行分类.

### 1.3 教学提示
> 通过列表等多种方式，对不同生物类群的形态结构、生活环境、繁殖方式等进行比较.

---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解植物的分类（藻→被子）的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解植物的分类（藻→被子）的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法
- 学生能够运用生物学学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验生物学探究的乐趣，树立学好数学的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 植物的分类（藻→被子）的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构
- 植物的分类（藻→被子）的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构

### 3.2 教学难点
- 掌握植物的分类（藻→被子）的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用
- 掌握植物的分类（藻→被子）的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
植物的主要类群是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“根据生物的形态结构、生理功能以及繁殖方式等，可以将生物分为不同的类群。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「植物的主要类群」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“根据生物的形态结构、生理功能以及繁殖方式等，可以将生物分为不同的类群。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

deep_textbook_snippets.text: Not surprisingly, the natural science of biology has many branches or subdisciplines. Cell biologists study cell structure and function, while biologists who study anatomy investigate the structure of an entire organism. Those biologists studying physiology, however, focus on the internal functioning of an organism. Some areas of biology focus on only particular types of living things. For example, botanists explore plants, while zoologists specialize in animals. Scientific Reasoning One thing is common to all forms of science: an ultimate goal “to know.” Curiosity and inquiry are the driving forces for the development of science. Scientists seek to understand the world and the way it operates. To do this, they use two methods of logical thinking: inductive reasoning and deductive reasoning. Inductive reasoning is a form of logical thinking that uses related observations to arrive at a general conclusion. This type of reasoning is common in descriptive science. A life scientist such as a biologist makes observations and records them. These data can be qualitative or quantitative, and one can supplement the raw data with drawings, pictures, photos, or videos. From many observations, the scientist can infer conclusions (inductions) based on evidence. Inductive reasoning involves formulating generalizations inferred from careful observation and analyzing a large amount of data. Brain studies provide an example. In this type of research, scientists observe many live brains while people are engaged in a specific activity, such as viewing images of food. The scientist then predic

When viewed together, these eight characteristics serve to define life. Order FIGURE 1.10 A toad represents a highly organized structure consisting of cells, tissues, organs, and organ systems. (credit: “Ivengo”/Wikimedia Commons) Organisms are highly organized, coordinated structures that consist of one or more cells. Even very simple, singlecelled organisms are remarkably complex: inside each cell, atoms form molecules. These in turn form cell organelles and other cellular inclusions. In multicellular organisms (Figure 1.10), similar cells form tissues. Tissues, in turn, collaborate to create organs (body structures with a distinct function). Organs work together to form organ systems. Sensitivity or Response to Stimuli FIGURE 1.11 The leaves of this sensitive plant (Mimosa pudica) will instantly droop and fold when touched. After a few minutes, the plant returns to normal. (credit: Alex Lomas) Organisms respond to diverse stimuli. For example, plants can bend toward a source of light, climb on fences and walls, or respond to touch (Figure 1.11). Even tiny bacteria can move toward or away from chemicals (a process called chemotaxis) or light (phototaxis). Movement toward a stimulus is a positive response, while movement away from a stimulus is a negative response.
Watch this video (https://openstax.org/l/movement_plants) to see how plants respond to a stimulus—from opening to light, to wrapping a tendril around a branch, to capturing prey. Reproduction Single-celled organisms reproduce by first duplicating their DNA, and then dividing it equally as the cell prepares to di

Watch this video (https://openstax.org/l/rotating_DNA) that animates the three-dimensional structure of the DNA molecule in Figure 1.15. Some cells contain aggregates of macromolecules surrounded by membranes. We call these organelles. Organelles are small structures that exist within cells. Examples of organelles include mitochondria and chloroplasts, which carry out indispensable functions: mitochondria process energy to power the cell, while chloroplasts enable green plants to utilize the energy in sunlight to make sugars. All living things are made of cells. The cell itself is the smallest fundamental unit of structure and function in living organisms. (This requirement is why scientists do not consider viruses living: they are not made of cells. To make new viruses, they have to invade and hijack the reproductive mechanism of a living cell. Only then can they obtain the materials they need to reproduce.) Some organisms consist of a single cell and others are multicellular. Scientists classify cells as prokaryotic or eukaryotic. Prokaryotes are single-celled or colonial organisms that do not have membrane-bound nuclei. In contrast, the cells of eukaryotes do have membrane-bound organelles and a membrane-bound nucleus. In larger organisms, cells combine to make tissues, which are groups of similar cells carrying out similar or related functions. Organs are collections of tissues grouped together performing a common function. Organs are present not only in animals but also in plants. An organ system is a higher level of organization that consists of functionally related o

> **[Figure 24, Page 43]** A large illustration or diagram (544×940px) related to: FIGURE, molecules, including, molecule, comprised, atoms. This image appears on page 43 of the textbook.
![Figure 24 - A large illustration or diagram (544×940px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0024_57bbb5c5.jpg)
Biologists collectively call all the individuals of a species living within a specific area a population. For example, a forest may include many pine trees, which represent the population of pine trees in this forest. Different populations may live in the same specific area. For example, the forest with the pine trees includes populations of flowering plants, insects, and microbial populations. A community is the sum of populations inhabiting a particular area. For instance, all of the trees, flowers, insects, and other populations in a forest form the forest’s community. The forest itself is an ecosystem. An ecosystem consists of all the living things in a particular area together with the abiotic, nonliving parts of that environment such as nitrogen in the soil or rain water. At the highest level of organization (Figure 1.16), the biosphere is the collection of all ecosystems, and it represents the zones of life on Earth. It includes land, water, and even the atmosphere to a certain extent.

deep_worked_example.stem: 根据教材片段，设计一道关于「植物的主要类群」的现象解释或实验探究题。
deep_worked_example.solution_outline: 先描述现象或实验条件，再指出关键概念和证据，最后给出解释或结论。
deep_worked_example.evidence_excerpt: Not surprisingly, the natural science of biology has many branches or subdisciplines. Cell biologists study cell structure and function, while biologists who study anatomy investigate the structure of an entire organism. Those biologists studying physiology, h

errors.description: 把「植物的主要类群」当作孤立事实记忆，不能说明结构、功能和生命过程之间的关系。
解释生命现象时缺少证据，容易把相关关系误认为因果关系。

exercises.stem: 用结构与功能或过程与结果的关系解释「植物的主要类群」。
exercises.answer: 应扣住课标要点：根据生物的形态结构、生理功能以及繁殖方式等，可以将生物分为不同的类群。，说明生命现象、结构/过程和功能意义。

exercises.stem: 举一个生活、健康或生态案例，说明「植物的主要类群」的应用价值。
exercises.answer: 需写出案例、涉及的生物学原理以及对生活或环境的意义。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「植物的主要类群」相关的生命现象。
